cloud-run.purpose: Deploy and verify the Cloud Run ai-engine within OpenManager free-tier and rollback constraints.
invariants: Check cost and free-tier guardrails before deploy.
Prefer GitLab CI deploy_ai_engine as the production deploy authority; direct Cloud Run deploy is an explicit fallback.
Verify Docker, type-check, service health, and rollback path.
Do not suggest higher-cost Cloud Build or Cloud Run machine settings.

code-review.purpose: Review OpenManager changes with severity-first findings and evidence-backed go/no-go judgment.
invariants: Findings come before summary.
Use correctness, readability, complexity, design, performance, security, and test coverage perspectives when relevant.
Do not claim safety without command evidence or an explicit testing gap.
Security and Correctness findings are auto-escalated one level due to vibe coding (AI-generated code only).

doc-management.purpose: Control documentation sprawl with budget, metadata, duplicate, stale-doc, and AI operating-doc drift checks.
invariants: Run docs budget checks before creating or broadening docs.
Run AI operating-doc consistency checks when MCP, skills, or agent guidance changes.
Prefer merging existing docs over adding new active docs.
Changed docs must include required metadata.

env-sync.purpose: Diagnose and fix environment drift across local, Vercel, Supabase, and Cloud Run boundaries.
invariants: Confirm the symptom is actually environment drift before changing code.
Use project sync scripts over ad hoc CLI updates.
Resolve Cloud Run service URLs from CLOUD_RUN_AI_URL or gcloud instead of hardcoding a live URL.
Do not expose secret values in output or committed files.

git-clean-gone.purpose: Remove local branches whose upstream is gone, handling worktree-attached branches safely.
invariants: Fetch and prune before deciding what is gone.
Never remove a worktree or branch without showing the candidate list.
Avoid destructive cleanup outside gone branch scope.

git-workflow.purpose: Commit, push, and PR safely using OpenManager canonical GitLab topology and conventional commits.
invariants: Inspect remotes before push or fetch.
Default push target is gitlab, not GitHub public snapshot.
After gitlab push, verify the pushed HEAD pipeline when GitLab token access is available.
Do not stage or revert unrelated user changes.

lint-smoke.purpose: Run fast local quality checks and report pass/fail evidence for changed OpenManager code.
invariants: Use scope to choose lightweight versus full smoke checks.
Run contract tests when API, auth, environment, or runtime contracts change.
Report skipped checks with a concrete reason.

qa-ops.purpose: Execute final QA with the right target environment and record every run into the QA tracker.
invariants: Use Vercel plus Playwright MCP by default for release-facing QA.
Switch to local dev only when AI-path validation is unnecessary.
Record QA evidence through qa:record and status commands.

For async AI Job + SSE QA, use the UI flow instead of CSRF-bypassing direct fetches, inspect EventSource streams through the Performance API, and report health probe failures separately from successful query execution.

qa-state.purpose: Thin wrapper that sequences state-triage before qa-ops when a request needs both current-state analysis and QA recording.
invariants: Do not duplicate detailed state-triage or qa-ops procedures.
Run state-triage first and only continue to qa-ops when QA is the selected next action.
Record QA outcomes through qa-ops and recommend the smallest next action.

state-triage.purpose: Analyze current QA, runtime, deployment, and AI-path evidence to choose the next action.
invariants: Load current policy and recent evidence before proposing fixes.
Classify the failure type before editing code.
Prefer the smallest code path or config path that explains the symptom.
